pre_registration.rationale: Attesting existing PASS evidence (T-015, validated 2026-03) under the new leverage-card/v1 schema. Not an independent re-validation; the original script and artifacts are unchanged.

objective.statement: Demonstrate intrinsic GR proper-time asymmetry over the best-fit flat-spacetime baseline across Kerr spin parameters a/M in {0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9}.
objective.metric_definition: abs(asymmetry_kerr - asymmetry_flat_best_fit), where asymmetry = (tau_prograde - tau_retrograde) / mean(tau)

intervention.description: Integrate Kerr equatorial geodesics for prograde and retrograde test particles and compute the proper-time asymmetry. Compare against the best-fit flat-space boost-velocity model.

knobs.flat_baseline: boost-velocity scan, best fit selected per spin

denominators.compute: single-process Python; ~minutes wall time on the original validation machine
denominators.evidence_cost: low — deterministic offline script with committed result JSON

reversibility.rollback_method: git revert; pure offline computation, no persistent state beyond the research artifact directory

failure_conditions: Residual < 5% across all spin parameters → asymmetry indistinguishable from flat-space best fit
Residual scales linearly with numerical integrator tolerance → numerical artifact rather than physical effect
Swapping prograde/retrograde labels reverses the sign of the residual → bookkeeping bug
Best-fit boost velocity drifts off the flat-space scan grid → flat-space comparator is malformed

observations.note: below 5% threshold; matches predicted near-flat limit

measured_outcome.summary: 4/5 spin conditions clear the 5% residual threshold; residual is monotonic in spin and matches the GR prediction. a/M=0.1 falls below threshold but is consistent with the predicted near-flat limit.

decision.rationale: Retrospective attestation of T-015 under leverage-card/v1. Effect size is large, monotonic in spin, and matches the GR prediction; near-flat exception at a/M=0.1 is consistent with the predicted small-spin limit rather than a failed claim. Not an independent re-validation.
